slides.headline: 101 Tempus Resource License Model 6 Components FULL USER This is the license for the individual pro users who do data entry in Tempus Resource. Typically, this corresponds to the sum of resource managers, project managers and the PMO. EXEC USER This is the license for the management level. It provides read-only access to Tempus modules and reports (excl. What-If’s). Note: If you utilize a reporting solution and/or Insight+, this license might be obsolete. TIMESHEET USER This license provides the employee with a view of the work/projects for which she/he is assigned to. In addition, the user can track hours via planned tasks. Submitted time sheet hours can then be approved or rejected by planners or resource managers (full users). Typically, the number of time sheet users matches the resource workgroup. RESOURCE WORKGROUP The size of the workgroup is defined by the number of resources that can be planned and controlled using Tempus Resource. Typically, this corresponds to the sum of named and generic resources. The workgroup is available in the following scales: 50, 100, 250, 500, 1’000, 2’500, 5’000, 7’500, 10’000, 20’000, etc.

slides.headline: Resource & Team Capacity Planning Maintain time-phased resource capacity in FTE/Hours . Resource Managers can create and view allocations on projects and business as usual activities. Spreadsheet-like interface for single and multiple project planning and staffing. Real-time, configurable, heatmapping highlighting overallocations by day, week, month, quarter, year, fiscal month, fiscal quarter. Flexible forecasting by role, named resource, teams, or other placeholders. Construct dynamic teams by assigning members and inclusion% by week/month/quarter. Multiple units of measuring capacity : hours, costs, FTE, FTE%, persondays , story points (for teams). Auto-calculate units of capacity in the UI between any of the available measures. Various planning granularities : day, week, month, quarter, year, fiscal month/quarter, project. Accurate workforce capacity planning

slides.headline: Resource Approval Workflows Comprehensive resource request and approval workflows . Resource replacement and resource approval interfaces provide 360° overview on resource attributes, skills, and remaining availability. AI-supported best fit resource recommendations based on resource metadata, skills, and remaining availability. Easily switch resource requests between units of capacity on various time granularities. Set up in-App or MS Teams notifications for different events in the workflow on a user-level. Resource replacement and resource approval support contoured replacement and partial replacement for placeholder resources. Global replacement with minimal clicks e.g., employee contract end or absences. Seamless resource approval and replacement

slides.headline: Scenario Modeling & Simulation Simulate project timeframe changes (canceling, cloning, expanding, contracting, shifting, and splitting). Identify optimal start and delivery dates for new and existing projects. Visualize resource pool impact on availability and capacity on an individual and aggregational-level via heatmaps, coolmaps , supply/demand graphics. Simulate headcount changes and resource replacements on projects, tasks, activities. Visualize financial impact on labour and non- labour costs against portfolio budgets. View non- labour costs by cost categories by project and by cost type. Multi-user environment with ability to create unlimited models (shared or private). No impact on live data with traceable actions that can be selectively undone. Real-time simulations on projects, resources, and financials

slides.headline: Skills Inventory Management Centrally manage comprehensive Skills Matrix comprising of 1000+ skills. Easily track skill levels using rankings, true/false, high-medium-low and proficiency values. Import skills via Excel, API or easily add them through the UI with pick lists and sliders. Flexible access policies for hierarchical management or individual user access i.e., skills can be edited by managers & end users can also self-submit their own skills assessment. Integrate skills data into reporting dashboards to identify gaps via heatmaps. Incorporate skills into resource planning activities by including skills data in resource approval and resource replacement screens. Integrating Skills into Resource Management

slides.headline: 41 Tempus License Model FULL USER This is the license for the individual professional users who change/update any data in Tempus Resource. Typically, this corresponds to the sum of resource managers, project managers and the PMO (incl. What-If’s). TIMESHEET USER This license provides the employee with a view of the work/projects for which they are assigned to. In addition, the user can track hours via planned tasks. Submitted time sheet hours can then be approved or rejected by planners or resource managers (full users). RESOURCE WORKGROUP The size of the workgroup is defined by the number of resources that can be planned and controlled using Tempus Resource. This count includes all active and inactive resources, all demand planning resources, and all system users regardless of license type. Scales: 50, 100, 250, 500, 1’000, 2’500, 5’000, 7’500, 10’000, 20’000, etc. INSIGHT+ The INSIGHT+ license provides the customer with an advanced integrated BI module, reports, dashboards and analytics. This is an application-level license. PREMIUM SUPPORT & SANDBOX This includes a development/test environment, which is regularly mirrored from the production environment and used for testing, integration, training. In addition, customers have priority in upgrades and access to specialists regarding data import. EXEC USER This is the license for the management level. It provides read-only access to Tempus modules and reports (excl. What-If’s). Note: If you utilize a reporting solution and/or Insight+, this license might be obsolete. TEAM RESOURCE This license allows you to assign named resources by teams, time-phased over time. A monthly individual % inclusion of the capacity of the named resources is also possible, as well as the automated calculation of the costs of the teams (per hour/person-day/story point). Scales: 5, 15, 30, 50, 100, 250, etc. TEMPUS PORTFOLIO Tempus Portfolio gives you full visibility into how your initiatives connect to your organization’s top-level goals, visions, strategies, objectives, OKRs, and investment targets (budgets).

slides.headline: Portf . Mgmt Proj . Mgmt Chg. Mgmt Res. Mgmt Team Mgr Team Mbr Upstream process Regular process Conflict Proceeding Create project in Tempus Approval OpsComm Plan Create Resource Request Process Resource Request Conflict Proceeding (Slide 8) Approve assignment Track Timesheet hours Plan & Create Resource Request Process Resource Request Conflict proceeding Approve assignment Track timesheet hours The Project Manager plans the tasks (no planning on generic tasks!) and assigns generic resources (namely resource assignments only in case of a project extension). Initially project plans are set up in the Demand Data Set in Tempus (at this stage no Resource Requests are generated). Once the projects have been approved in the OpsComm , the Demand Data Set will be transferred to the Allocation Data Set , which generates Resource Requests and therefore activates the further Resource Planning Process . A Resource Request will be generated automatically each time of a task assignment or an update of an assigned task. T he responsible Team Manager verifies at least once a week all planned tasks and the estimated efforts for his/her Team Members, while processing pending resource requests, Resource requests are processed in the following order: 1. Time relevance 2. status of the request in the following order: implement, plan, initiate, (approved*). While processing requests, the demand priority needs to be considered first. When processing the resource requests, the Team Manager checks whether the requested efforts are valid and correspond to the scope of the task. For Generic Resource Requests, t he Team Manager assigns a namely resource from his team considering the resource availability during the requested time period and the skills needed to perform the respective tasks. Conflict proceeding involves the following roles: Resource Manager , Team Manager, Project Manager , Portfolio Manager . Via Tempus Resource Request, t he responsible Team Manager approves the planned effort and time period for a namely assigned resource. Once a resource request is assigned to a namely resource, the planned efforts are visible in the timesheets of the assigned team member. At least once a week the Team Member tracks his weekly efforts made to perform the planned project tasks via My Timesheet in Tempus. The Team Member contacts the Team Manger or Project Manager in case of discrepancies (e.g. tasks are requested that are not visible in the timesheets). weekly weekly Resource Management Process Portfolio Management Project Management Change Management Resource Management Team Manager ( Führungskraft ) Team/Project Member Portf .
